metrics.title: Q1 2026 EPS
metrics.label: Beat by 9.6%; +43% YoY from $0.96 — data center momentum continues

moat.description: Strong positioning as the primary x86 alternative and growing software (ROCm) ecosystem.

analysisPoints.title: Chiplet Innovation
analysisPoints.text: AMD led the transition to chiplets, allowing for higher yields and more flexible SKU creation compared to monolithic designs.

analysisPoints.title: x86 Market Share Capture
analysisPoints.text: Continues to erode Intel's dominance in the server (EPYC) and consumer (Ryzen) markets.

analysisPoints.title: Open Ecosystem
analysisPoints.text: ROCm software suite is becoming a viable open-source alternative to Nvidia's proprietary CUDA, attracting hyperscalers looking for vendor flexibility.

growth.description: Q1 2026 EPS $1.37 beat consensus by ~10% (+43% YoY); the OpenAI 6 GW compute deal, multi-year Meta partnership, and the MI450/Helios ramp into 2H 2026 are driving consensus upgrades and a 60%+ YTD stock rally.

growthAnalysis.scoreDerivation: Base 88 (28-38% CAGR mid-band) + 6 Q2 guide implying +46% YoY + 5 Data Center +57% YoY and OpenAI/Meta deals + 3 expanding gross margin - 9 NVIDIA Rubin Ultra competitive overhang = 93

drivers.name: Data Center / Instinct GPUs
drivers.metric: Q1 2026 Data Center revenue $5.8B (+57% YoY); MI450 + Helios volume ramp 2H 2026; OpenAI 6 GW commitment

drivers.name: EPYC Server CPUs
drivers.metric: Server share compounding past 35% as hyperscalers diversify; embedded in Data Center segment alongside GPUs

drivers.name: Client / Ryzen
drivers.metric: Ryzen retail share ~46% in Q4; consumer client revenue stable with AI-PC mix lift

growthAnalysis.keyRisk: If NVIDIA's Rubin Ultra widens the performance gap on flagship training workloads through 2027 and ROCm fails to close the developer-ecosystem gap to CUDA, AMD's AI GPU share stalls below 15% of TAM and the 28-38% CAGR collapses toward 15-20% — invalidating the consensus upgrade cycle priced in after the OpenAI deal.

valuation.description: At ~$356, AMD trades just below the revised base ($390) on the back of the OpenAI 6 GW commitment and Meta multi-year deal — fair-to-modestly-rich entry, with risk/reward improving if 2H 2026 MI450/Helios execution lands as guided.

bear.description: AI capex moderates in 2027, NVIDIA reasserts dominance with Rubin Ultra, and the OpenAI/Meta deals scale back materially.
points: Hyperscaler AI capex growth decelerates from 40%+ to mid-teens as training-cluster overbuild concerns surface
Rubin Ultra performance leadership widens vs. MI450, capping AMD's share of new AI GPU spend at <15%
Intel 18A regains incremental server CPU share, slowing EPYC's compounding

base.description: MI450/Helios ramps to volume in 2H 2026, OpenAI 6 GW deal deploys on schedule, and AMD compounds AI GPU share toward 20% of TAM.
points: AI GPU revenue reaches $25–30B in FY2026 as OpenAI, Meta, and Microsoft scale MI450 deployments
EPYC server share crosses 35%, with hyperscaler vendor diversification accelerating
Operating margin expands to 30%+ as AI mix and software (ROCm) adoption improves pricing

bull.description: AMD becomes a credible second-source AI standard, capturing 25%+ of AI GPU spend as ROCm reaches functional parity with CUDA for inference and key training workloads.
points: MI500 roadmap (2027) achieves performance parity with NVIDIA on flagship training workloads
OpenAI 6 GW commitment scales to 10 GW with multi-year extension, and a second hyperscaler signs a similar deal
ROCm crosses 1M+ developers and becomes the default open alternative for inference workloads

learnedInterfaces.note: GPU programming interfaces are commoditized; ROCm still significantly lags CUDA in developer tooling.

businessLogic.note: Not applicable as a primary competitive moat for semiconductor design.

publicDataAccess.note: Not applicable to AMD's competitive position.

talentScarcity.note: Chip design engineers remain scarce; Lisa Su's executive team is world-class and hard to replicate.

bundling.note: AMD sells chips, not full-stack AI infrastructure solutions — limited bundling moat vs. NVIDIA's CUDA ecosystem.

proprietaryData.note: CDNA architecture IP, ROCm optimization data, and foundry partnership integration data remain proprietary.

regulatoryLockIn.note: Limited government contract footprint compared to NVIDIA; some defense wins but not entrenched.

networkEffects.note: ROCm developer ecosystem is a fraction of CUDA's 4M+ developer community — the critical gap to close.

transactionEmbedding.note: Embedded in hyperscaler data centers as a second-source alternative, with growing MI300X adoption at Microsoft.

systemOfRecord.note: Not yet the default standard; AMD operates in NVIDIA's shadow in AI compute despite superior price/performance in some workloads.

tenMoats.verdict: AMD's moat is weakening in AI-resilient categories vs. NVIDIA. Execution quality is world-class, but CUDA's network effect remains the dominant barrier — AMD's upside is a CUDA challenger, not a CUDA replacer.
